headline: Why the Hormuz 'mission' led by France and the UK is largely symbolic
description: Wanting to avoid being drawn into the US's blockade of the key shipping route, Macron and Starmer aim to establish a multilateral and 'strictly defensive' plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
French businesses will (mostly) remain closed on Labor Day as government backs down
Sarkozy loyalist hits back after ex-president's attacks at appeal trial over alleged Libyan funding
description: Claude Guéant, too ill to attend the appeal hearings into suspicions of Libyan funding for Nicolas Sarkozy's 2007 presidential campaign, sent a terse three-page letter, marking in a turning point in the trial.

headline: Sepideh Farsi, filmmaker: 'We are witnessing the destruction of Iranian heritage, culture and environment'
description: The Iranian director shares her anguish over the consequences of American airstrikes on her country. Beyond the loss of life and material devastation, she reflects on the wonders of Iranian civilization and culture that have been permanently scarred.

headline: Calder at the Fondation Louis Vuitton in Paris, a festive experience
description: The exhibition spans the sculptor's entire body of work and takes over the entire building, spilling out onto the exterior. The journey is a joyful celebration, filled with lightness and movement.

headline: 'At that price, how can we accept?' Trump Organization's Vietnam golf project sparks protests and corruption allegations
description: Signed just before the 2024 US presidential election, the project has caused divisions within the government and sparked anger among local residents, who have been offered extremely low compensation for the seizure of their land.

Will California's next governor be a Republican?
description: The crowded field of Democratic candidates running in the gubernatorial election raises the possibility that a Trump-aligned Republican could win in November in this deeply progressive state.

headline: American Catholics outraged at Trump's attack of pope and AI-generated image of himself as Christ
description: The US president claimed, against all plausibility, that the image he posted on social media showed him as a doctor, but he was forced to take it down. The post, along with his lengthy tirade against Leo XIV, left believers stunned.

headline: New online search tool in Germany reveals if ancestors were Nazi Party members
description: In partnership with American and German archives, the weekly 'Die Zeit' has launched a searchable tool granting access to more than 12 million documents, cataloging the bulk of Nazi Party (NSDAP) memberships from 1925 to 1945.
